ucs.title: [LLM] APT29 / Midnight Blizzard OAuth illicit consent grant & malicious application registration
ucs.description: Hunts APT29's hallmark cloud post-compromise tradecraft: registering or consenting to a malicious OAuth application in Entra ID (Azure AD) to harvest mail and persist beyond credential resets - the exact pattern used in the SolarWinds/Nobelium intrusions and the 2023-2024 Microsoft corporate mailbox compromise.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Change.object) as object values(Change.user) as actor values(Change.src) as src from datamodel=Change where Change.action=created OR Change.action=modified (Change.object_category="application" OR Change.object_category="service_principal" OR Change.change_type="AAD") by Change.object_id Change.user Change.result | `drop_dm_object_name(Change)` | join type=inner object_id [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Change where Change.action="Consent to application" OR Change.action="Add app role assignment" OR Change.action="Add delegated permission grant" by Change.object_id Change.vendor_product | `drop_dm_object_name(Change)`] | search (action="Consent to application" OR object IN ("Mail.Read","Mail.ReadWrite","Mail.Send","full_access_as_app","Files.Read.All","Directory.Read.All")) | eval risk="APT29 OAuth consent grant pattern" | table firstTime lastTime actor src object_id object risk
ucs.kql:
let suspiciousScopes = dynamic(["Mail.Read","Mail.ReadWrite","Mail.Send","MailboxSettings.ReadWrite","full_access_as_app","Files.Read.All","Sites.Read.All","Directory.Read.All","EWS.AccessAsUser.All"]);
CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where ActionType in ("Consent to application.","Add app role assignment to service principal.","Add delegated permission grant.","Add service principal.","Add application.","Add owner to application.","Update application – Certificates and secrets management")
| extend props = parse_json(RawEventData)
| extend ModifiedProps = tostring(props.ModifiedProperties), TargetApp = tostring(props.Target[0].ID), Actor = tostring(AccountDisplayName)
| where ModifiedProps has_any (suspiciousScopes) or ActionType == "Update application – Certificates and secrets management"
| join kind=leftouter (
    AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where isnotempty(IPAddress)
    | summarize SignInIPs=make_set(IPAddress,50), SignInCountries=make_set(Country,20) by AccountObjectId
) on $left.AccountObjectId == $right.AccountObjectId
| extend RiskNote = "APT29/Midnight Blizzard – malicious OAuth app or secret added with mail/graph scopes"
| project Timestamp, Actor, AccountObjectId, ActionType, TargetApp, ModifiedProps, IPAddress, SignInIPs, SignInCountries, RiskNote
ucs.rationale: APT29 is the only major nation-state actor that has repeatedly weaponized OAuth consent grants and service-principal credential additions as its primary cloud persistence mechanism (MagicWeb, MagicWebShell, the Microsoft midnight-blizzard mailbox breach Jan 2024, HPE breach May 2023). Generic detections fire on any new app; this one specifically chains application creation with high-privilege Mail

ucs.title: [LLM] APT29 password-spray from residential proxy followed by ROADtools-style enumeration
ucs.description: Detects the specific Midnight Blizzard initial-access chain disclosed by Microsoft (MSRC Jan-2024): low-and-slow password spray (T1110.003) sourced from residential/anonymizing proxies (TOR, NordVPN, residential botnets), succeeding against a non-MFA legacy account, immediately followed by Graph/Azure AD reconnaissance against users, applications, and tenant settings.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count dc(Authentication.user) as uniqUsers values(Authentication.user) as users values(Authentication.src) as src values(Authentication.app) as app from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action=failure (Authentication.signature_id="50126" OR Authentication.signature_id="50053" OR Authentication.authentication_method="Password") earliest=-1d by Authentication.src_ip Authentication.user_agent _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)` | where uniqUsers >= 8 | lookup tor_exit_nodes ip as src_ip OUTPUT is_tor | lookup residential_proxy_ranges cidr as src_ip OUTPUT proxy_provider | where is_tor="true" OR isnotnull(proxy_provider) | join type=inner src_ip [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action=success by Authentication.src_ip Authentication.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)` | rename user as compromised_user] | eval threat="APT29 password spray from anon proxy with successful auth" | table _time src_ip user_agent proxy_provider is_tor uniqUsers compromised_user app threat
ucs.kql:
let window = 1d;
let sprayThreshold = 8;
let failedSprays =
    AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(window)
    | where ErrorCode in (50126, 50053, 50055, 50056, 50064)
    | summarize FailedUsers = dcount(AccountObjectId), Users = make_set(AccountUpn, 50), UAs = make_set(UserAgent, 10), Countries = make_set(Country, 10)
        by IPAddress, bin(Timestamp, 1h)
    | where FailedUsers >= sprayThreshold;
let success =
    AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(window)
    | where ErrorCode == 0
    | project SuccessTime=Timestamp, IPAddress, SuccessAccountUpn=AccountUpn, SuccessAccountObjectId=AccountObjectId, Application, ResourceDisplayName, SessionId;
failedSprays
| join kind=inner success on IPAddress
| where SuccessTime between (Timestamp .. (Timestamp + 2h))
| join kind=leftouter (
    CloudAppEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(window)
    | where ActionType in ("List users.","List applications.","List service principals.","List directory roles.","Get-AzureADUser","Get-AzureADApplication","Get-MgUser","Get-MgApplication")
        or RawEventData has_any ("users?$select","servicePrincipals","applications?$","directoryRoles")
    | project ReconTime=Timestamp, SuccessAccountObjectId=AccountObjectId, ReconAction=ActionType
) on SuccessAccountObjectId
| where ReconTime between (SuccessTime .. (SuccessTime + 6h))
| extend Verdict = "APT29/Midnight Blizzard pattern: residential-proxy spray → success → Graph enumeration"
| project Timestamp, IPAddress, FailedUsers, Countries, UAs, SuccessAccountUpn, Application, SuccessTime, ReconTime, ReconAction, Verdict
ucs.rationale: Microsoft's Jan-2024 MSRC disclosure explicitly named this chain as Midnight Blizzard tradecraft: spray a non-MFA test/legacy tenant from a residential botnet, then immediately enumerate via Graph using ROADtools/AzureHound. The combination of (a) low-volume spray from anonymizing infra and (b) tightly-coupled post-auth directory enumeration within the same session is far more APT29-specific than
